scope.summary:
OPERATOR: launch a TERMINAL session that opens INSIDE a repo/workspace folder with the CORRECT shell wrapper, from the worksurface — the VS-Code 'open an integrated terminal in this repo' affordance. This makes Handshake usable as a dev tool (run commands in the project folder).

VERIFIED STATE (live-app inspection 2026-06-27): a REAL terminal backend EXISTS — src/backend/handshake_core/src/terminal/ (TerminalRuntime, TerminalRequest{cwd, command, args}, TerminalSession, TerminalSessionType{HumanDev/AiJob/PluginTool}) + terminal/pty.rs (PtySession, PtySpawnConfig) + terminal/runtime.rs + terminal/guards.rs (capability gating). It binds the repo/workspace folder via TerminalRequest.cwd and the shell wrapper via TerminalRequest.command + args. BUT it is reachable ONLY via Tauri IPC (app/src-tauri/src/commands/terminal.rs: kernel_terminal_create_session / write_stdin / resize / close / run_command / context / diagnostics) — there is NO HTTP /terminal route on handshake_core, and the NATIVE handshake_native frontend has NO terminal surface and NO native client for it. So the WORKSURFACE-LAUNCH-FROM-NATIVE path is GENUINELY ABSENT.

What to do (wire/expose natively if a reachable route exists, else an HONEST typed blocker — NOT a mock terminal):
- INSPECT terminal/mod.rs + terminal/runtime.rs + terminal/pty.rs to confirm the TerminalRuntime spawn API (cwd + command/args wrapper) and whether it is reachable from the native frontend process (it is an in-process Rust API in handshake_core, but handshake_native is a SEPARATE process that talks to handshake_core over HTTP — so confirm whether a HTTP terminal route exists; verified ABSENT today).
- ADD a native 'Open Terminal in repo folder' affordance from the worksurface: a top-menu / command-palette action 'Terminal: Open in Workspace Folder' + a TerminalLaunchClient (backend_client.rs) that issues the spawn request to the REAL route IF one exists. The request MUST carry (a) the chosen repo/workspace folder as cwd and (b) the correct shell wrapper (the platform shell — pwsh/cmd on Windows, the configured wrapper) as command/args.
- BECAUSE the HTTP terminal route is VERIFIED ABSENT, the launch action records a TYPED BLOCKER: a typed `TerminalLaunchError::EndpointMissing` from TerminalLaunchClient::production() whose ctor documents the absent HTTP route (the PTY runtime exists but is IPC-only), and the affordance shows an HONEST disabled/blocked state ('Native terminal launch needs a handshake_core HTTP /terminal/spawn route or the Tauri IPC bridge — PTY runtime exists in terminal/ but is not reachable from the native frontend'). DO NOT spawn a fake PTY or fabricate terminal output. If, on inspection, a reachable route is found (a HTTP /terminal route, or a same-process embedding of TerminalRuntime in the native binary), wire the launch -> real spawn and prove the cwd lands in the chosen repo folder with the wrapper instead.
- CREATE src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_terminal_launch.rs.

Reuse notes: the terminal PTY runtime (cwd + wrapper) is BUILT (terminal/**); the reuse-via-API surface is backend_client.rs; the command-palette / top-menu affordance is the existing WP-011 command surface. src/backend untouched (reuse-via-API only; the PTY runtime is reference for the request shape).

Risk if missed: a mock terminal (fake output, no real PTY) would violate the no-mock Spec-Realism Gate; an absent native route must be an HONEST typed blocker naming the existing PTY runtime + the missing reach, not a fabricated terminal.
forbidden_paths: src/backend/** (reuse via API only — the terminal PTY runtime exists; do not edit it)
src/backend/handshake_core/src/terminal/** (reference for the request shape only; reuse via API)
app/src/** (legacy React + Tauri IPC, reference only)
acceptance_criteria: AC-100-1 (WORKSURFACE LAUNCH AFFORDANCE, prove first): a native 'Terminal: Open in Workspace Folder' action exists on the worksurface (command palette / top menu) and is discoverable — proven by a kittest asserting the command is registered + addressable (a stable author_id / command id) and invoking it routes to the TerminalLaunchClient.

AC-100-2 (REAL SPAWN IN THE REPO FOLDER WITH THE WRAPPER, OR HONEST TYPED BLOCKER): invoking the action issues a spawn request carrying the chosen repo/workspace folder as cwd + the correct shell wrapper (command/args) to the REAL route. EITHER (a) a reachable route exists and a launched terminal session lands its cwd in the chosen repo folder with the wrapper — proven by a test observing the spawned session's resolved cwd == the repo folder + the wrapper command (live, against the real runtime); OR (b) the native HTTP terminal route is verified ABSENT and the launch returns a typed `TerminalLaunchError::EndpointMissing` and the affordance shows an HONEST blocked state naming the existing PTY runtime + the missing native reach — proven by a test asserting the typed-blocker path (NO fabricated PTY/output). Exactly one of (a)/(b), by the live backend (verified ABSENT today => path (b) expected).

AC-100-3 (NO MOCK): no fake terminal output, no fabricated PTY, no synthesized cwd-resolution; the cwd + wrapper are either really resolved by the runtime (path a) or the launch is a typed blocker (path b).

AC-100-4: `cargo build -p handshake-native` (lib + bins) clean; clippy `--all-targets -D warnings` clean; full suite `-j 2` green; external CARGO_TARGET_DIR; no Color32 literal outside theme/palette.rs (CONTROL-4); `git ls-files src/**/*.png` empty.

AC-100-LIVE (LIVE TERMINAL LAUNCH VIA THE OPERATOR SURFACE): with handshake_core running, driving the operator surface opens a real terminal session whose cwd is the chosen repo/workspace folder with the correct wrapper; proven via the GUI (visual debugger / foreground-safe nav) + a screenshot; or an explicit typed blocker naming the missing native->backend terminal route (NOT a faked terminal).
proof_targets: PT-100-A: kittest asserts the 'Terminal: Open in Workspace Folder' command is registered + addressable + routes to the TerminalLaunchClient on invoke.

PT-100-B: test proves EITHER a real spawn lands cwd == the chosen repo folder + the wrapper command (live runtime), OR the typed EndpointMissing blocker + honest blocked state (no fabricated PTY). One or the other, by the live backend.
PT-100-C: cargo build --lib --bins clean + clippy -D warnings clean + full suite -j 2 green + CONTROL-4 green + git ls-files src/**/*.png empty.
implementation_notes: OPERATOR STEER 2026-06-27 - LIVE OPERATOR-SURFACE TEST: actually launch a TERMINAL session THROUGH the operator surface (the running GUI) and prove the shell opens with its cwd INSIDE the chosen repo/workspace folder using the correct wrapper. Start handshake_core (127.0.0.1:37501) for the live test; drive the launch via the MT-102 visual debugger + MT-103 FOREGROUND-SAFE nav (no OS mouse/keyboard/foreground steal) + capture a screenshot. If the native operator surface cannot reach the PTY runtime today (terminal/ runtime is Tauri-IPC-only, no native HTTP /terminal route, no native terminal client), surface a CLEAR typed blocker naming the EXACT missing native->backend terminal route as a backend follow-up - do NOT fake an opened terminal.
Use the EXTERNAL CARGO_TARGET_DIR (Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-native-target); NEVER a repo-local target/. Run the full suite with -j 2.

The PTY runtime EXISTS (terminal/mod.rs TerminalRuntime + terminal/pty.rs PtySession; TerminalRequest carries cwd + command/args = the wrapper). It is reachable today ONLY via Tauri IPC (app/src-tauri kernel_terminal_create_session), NOT via a handshake_core HTTP route, and the native frontend is a SEPARATE process with no terminal client. So the native worksurface launch is a TYPED BLOCKER (EndpointMissing) naming the existing runtime + the missing HTTP/IPC reach — NOT a mock.

HONESTY (no-mock Spec-Realism Gate): do NOT spawn a fake PTY or fabricate terminal output / a fake resolved cwd. The typed blocker is the MT-079/080 EndpointMissing pattern. If on inspection a reachable route is genuinely found (a HTTP /terminal route, or the native binary can embed TerminalRuntime in-process), wire the real spawn + prove the cwd/wrapper instead (prefer the real wire).

The wrapper is the platform shell + the project's configured shell wrapper (pwsh/cmd on Windows, the configured wrapper). Carry it as TerminalRequest.command + args. The repo/workspace folder is the active workspace's folder (the cwd).
depends_on MT-097 (the worksurface must exist to launch from). blocks MT-104 (the manual documents launching a terminal in a repo folder).
Read the KERNEL_BUILDER coder protocol under .GOV/ first; the Spec-Realism Gate forbids a mock terminal — AC-100-2/3 require a real spawn or an honest typed blocker.
ports_from_react: app/src/components/terminal/TerminalPanel.tsx + app/src/lib/ipc/terminal.ts (the React terminal panel + IPC are REFERENCE for the request shape only; the native backend HTTP terminal route is absent).
binds_backend_api: ATTEMPTED: a terminal spawn route carrying cwd (repo folder) + command/args (wrapper). The PTY runtime EXISTS (terminal/**) but is reachable only via Tauri IPC, NOT a handshake_core HTTP route, and the native frontend has no terminal client. So the launch is a typed EndpointMissing blocker until a HTTP /terminal route (or an in-process embedding) exists. No new backend route built here. PostgreSQL/EventLedger only; no SQLite.

hbr_int_009_tier_obligations.reason: This MT adds a native launch affordance + a typed-blocker client; it does not read or write the native Flight Recorder. (The backend terminal runtime has its own FR audit, but that is backend-owned and out of this MT's native scope.)
This MT does not emit internal_diagnostics events; it is a launch affordance + a typed EndpointMissing blocker.
No freeze/crash/debris record path is touched by adding a terminal-launch affordance.

handoff.coder_session: KERNEL_BUILDER-20260630-064524 product_commit=117fb78d; tests=test_terminal_launch 4/4, test_manual_content 7/7, test_top_menu_bar 13/13, test_command_palette 8/8, test_manual_pane_editors 8/8; post_review_pass=Curie,Meitner; residual=active workspace folder currently process cwd until canonical workspace filesystem root lands in MT-101

risks: RISK-100-1: a mock terminal (fake output, fabricated cwd) because the native route is absent — violates the no-mock Spec-Realism Gate. Mitigate: AC-100-2/3 require a real spawn OR a typed EndpointMissing blocker, never a fabricated PTY.

RISK-100-2: the spawn request omits the cwd or wrapper, so a real launch would not land in the repo folder. Mitigate: AC-100-2 asserts the request carries cwd == the repo folder + the wrapper command.
RISK-100-3: the affordance is built but not addressable/discoverable on the worksurface. Mitigate: AC-100-1 asserts the command is registered + addressable.
RISK-100-4: a repo-local target/ or tracked PNG leaks. Mitigate: external target dir; git ls-files src/**/*.png empty (AC-100-4).

RISK-100-5: the typed blocker is vague (does not name the existing PTY runtime + the missing reach), so the follow-on can't scope the real wire. Mitigate: the EndpointMissing blocker names terminal/** + the absent HTTP/IPC reach.
minimum_controls: MC-100-1: AC-100-1 proves a discoverable worksurface launch affordance.
MC-100-2: AC-100-2/3 require a real spawn (cwd==repo folder + wrapper) OR an honest typed EndpointMissing blocker naming the existing runtime — never a mock.
MC-100-3: external target + no tracked PNG + CONTROL-4 + full suite -j 2 green (AC-100-4).

research_provenance.source: WP-KERNEL-012 product-vision steer (operator, 2026-06-27); live-app inspection (terminal PTY runtime verified PRESENT in src/backend/handshake_core/src/terminal/**; reachable only via Tauri IPC; no handshake_core HTTP /terminal route; native frontend has no terminal surface).
research_provenance.field_stack: VS Code / Cursor integrated terminal: open a shell in the active repo/workspace folder with the configured shell wrapper. Reuses the existing handshake_core terminal PTY runtime (TerminalRequest cwd + command/args wrapper) as the request-shape reference + backend_client.rs reuse-via-API + the WP-011 command-palette/top-menu affordance. No new backend route.
research_provenance.motivating_incident: Handshake must be usable as a dev tool: launch a terminal in the repo folder with the correct wrapper from the worksurface. The native frontend has no terminal surface and the PTY runtime is IPC-only today.
research_provenance.validation_plan: discoverable worksurface launch affordance + a real spawn (cwd==repo folder + wrapper) OR an honest typed EndpointMissing blocker naming the existing PTY runtime + the missing native reach (no mock); clippy -D warnings; full suite -j 2.
